title: Ice Maker Fan Error
shortDescription: The ice maker fan motor is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: The Er IF error code indicates the ice maker blower fan motor has failed or is not spinning at the expected speed. This fan circulates cold air around the ice maker to facilitate ice production. Without it, ice production slows or stops. The fan may be blocked by ice buildup, have a failed motor, or have a wiring issue.

causes.cause: Ice buildup blocking the fan blades
Control board unable to send power to the fan

symptoms: Er IF displayed on the control panel
Ice production has stopped or slowed significantly
Freezer temperature may be slightly warmer than set
No airflow sound near the ice maker area

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes and plug it back in. Sometimes ice around the fan melts enough during the reset to allow it to spin freely again.

fixSteps.title: Defrost the ice maker area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all ice from the ice bin. Use a hair dryer on low heat to carefully melt any ice buildup around the ice maker fan area. Do not use boiling water as it can crack plastic components.

fixSteps.title: Check the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the ice maker assembly and back panel inside the freezer to access the fan. Try spinning the fan blade by hand. It should rotate freely. If it is stiff or does not spin, the motor bearings may be seized.

fixSteps.title: Test or replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the fan motor wiring. Test the motor with a multimeter for continuity (should read 40-100 ohms). If open, replace the fan motor. If it has continuity but does not spin, the bearings are failed.

partsNeeded.part: Ice Maker Assembly (if fan is integrated)

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor needs replacement and the ice maker assembly is difficult to access, or if ice buildup keeps recurring indicating a defrost system issue.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er IF ice maker fan error fix

faq.q: Why does ice build up around the fan?
faq.a: Ice buildup around the ice maker fan is usually caused by warm moist air entering the freezer (door left open, worn door gasket) or a defrost system issue that is not fully clearing frost.

faq.q: Can I still use the refrigerator with Er IF?
faq.a: Yes, the refrigerator and freezer will continue to cool. Only ice production is affected. You can manually make ice with trays.

faq.q: How do I prevent ice buildup around the ice maker?
faq.a: Ensure the freezer door seals properly, do not leave the door open for extended periods, and make sure the defrost system is working correctly.

title: Freezer Fan Error
shortDescription: The freezer evaporator fan motor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The Er FF error code indicates the freezer evaporator fan motor is not operating correctly. This fan is critical for circulating cold air throughout the freezer and refrigerator compartments. Without it, both sections will warm up. The fan motor may have failed, be blocked by ice, or have a wiring issue.

causes.cause: Ice or frost buildup on the evaporator coils blocking the fan
Defrost system failure causing excessive frost that blocks the fan

symptoms: Er FF displayed on the control panel
Freezer and refrigerator temperatures rising
No air circulation sound from the freezer
Frost or ice visible on the back wall of the freezer
Food thawing in the freezer

fixSteps.title: Check for ice buildup
fixSteps.instruction: Open the freezer and listen for the fan. If you hear no fan running, check the back wall of the freezer for heavy frost or ice. If there is significant ice buildup, the defrost system may have failed.

fixSteps.title: Manual defrost
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator and leave both doors open for 24 hours to completely defrost the evaporator. Place towels around the base to catch water. This will melt any ice blocking the fan.

fixSteps.title: Test the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: After defrosting, remove the freezer back panel (interior) to access the evaporator fan. Plug the refrigerator back in and observe if the fan runs. If it does not start within a few minutes, disconnect power and test the motor with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the fan motor mounting screws and disconnect the wiring harness. Transfer the fan blade to the new motor (if not included). Install the new motor, reconnect wiring, and reassemble the back panel.

whenToCallPro: If the defrost system has failed (causing recurring ice buildup), professional diagnosis of the defrost heater, thermostat, and timer is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er FF freezer fan error fix

faq.q: Is Er FF an emergency?
faq.a: Yes, without the freezer fan, temperatures in both the freezer and refrigerator will rise. Move perishable items to a cooler and address the issue promptly.

faq.q: Can I speed up the defrost?
faq.a: You can use a hair dryer on low heat to carefully melt ice around the evaporator, but never use sharp tools to chip ice as you could damage the evaporator coils.

faq.q: Why does ice keep building up on the evaporator?
faq.a: Recurring ice buildup indicates a defrost system failure. The defrost heater, defrost thermostat, or defrost control board may need replacement.

title: Condenser Fan Error
shortDescription: The condenser fan motor is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: The Er CF error code indicates the condenser fan motor has failed or is not running at the expected speed. The condenser fan is located near the compressor at the bottom rear of the refrigerator and is essential for dissipating heat from the refrigeration system. Without it, the compressor will overheat and the refrigerator will not cool properly.

causes.cause: Debris (dust, pet hair) blocking the fan blades
Disconnected or damaged fan motor wiring
Control board not sending power to the fan

symptoms: Er CF displayed on the control panel
Refrigerator not cooling properly
Compressor runs hot and may shut off
No fan noise from the bottom rear of the refrigerator
Compressor area is very hot to the touch

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser area
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator. Remove the back access panel at the bottom rear. Using a vacuum with a brush attachment, clean all dust, lint, and pet hair from around the condenser fan, condenser coils, and the surrounding area.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the condenser fan blades for any objects blocking rotation. Remove any debris. Try spinning the fan by hand; it should rotate freely.

fixSteps.title: Check fan motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring connection to the condenser fan motor. Ensure the connector is firmly seated and there are no damaged or chewed wires (common in homes with pets or rodents).

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Test the fan motor with a multimeter for continuity. If it fails, remove the mounting screws, disconnect the wiring, transfer the fan blade to the new motor, and install.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor is not the issue and the control board may be at fault.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er CF condenser fan error fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the condenser area?
faq.a: LG recommends cleaning the condenser coils and fan area every 6-12 months, more often if you have pets that shed.

faq.q: Can a dirty condenser fan cause other problems?
faq.a: Yes, a blocked condenser fan causes the compressor to overheat, which can lead to compressor failure, the most expensive refrigerator repair.

faq.q: Is the condenser fan the same as the evaporator fan?
faq.a: No. The condenser fan is at the bottom rear near the compressor (hot side). The evaporator fan is inside the freezer (cold side). They serve different purposes.

title: Compressor Error
shortDescription: The refrigerator compressor has a startup or operational failure.
fullDescription: The Er CO error code indicates the compressor has failed to start or is not operating within normal parameters. The compressor is the heart of the refrigeration system, pumping refrigerant through the system to cool the refrigerator. This is one of the most serious error codes as the refrigerator cannot cool without a functioning compressor. LG uses a linear compressor in many models which has specific failure modes.

causes.cause: Compressor start relay or overload protector failure
Low refrigerant due to a sealed system leak
Main control board not sending proper voltage to compressor

symptoms: Er CO displayed on the control panel
Refrigerator not cooling at all
Compressor not running (no humming or vibrating)
Clicking sounds from the compressor area as it tries to start
Both refrigerator and freezer temperatures rising

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator for 15 minutes. This allows the compressor's thermal overload protector to reset. Plug it back in and listen for the compressor to start (a low humming sound from the back).

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser coils
fixSteps.instruction: Dirty condenser coils force the compressor to work harder, leading to overheating and shutdowns. Clean the coils and surrounding area with a vacuum and brush.

fixSteps.title: Check the start relay
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator. Access the compressor at the bottom rear. Locate the start relay (a small component plugged onto the compressor). Remove it and shake it gently. If it rattles, the internal contacts have broken and it needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test compressor windings
fixSteps.instruction: With the start relay removed, use a multimeter to test resistance between the three compressor pins (common, start, run). Each pair should show 2-15 ohms. Also test from each pin to the compressor body (should be no continuity). Abnormal readings indicate compressor failure.

whenToCallPro: If the start relay and overload protector test fine, the compressor itself may need replacement. Compressor replacement involves working with refrigerant and requires EPA certification. Always use a licensed technician. Check if the compressor is under LG's extended warranty.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er CO compressor error fix start relay

faq.q: Is LG compressor failure common?
faq.a: LG linear compressors have had higher failure rates in some model years. LG has extended the compressor warranty to 10 years on many models. Check your warranty status before paying for repair.

faq.q: How much does compressor replacement cost?
faq.a: A compressor replacement typically costs $600-1200 professionally, including labor and refrigerant. However, LG may cover the compressor part under their extended warranty, reducing cost to labor only.

faq.q: Should I repair or replace the refrigerator?
faq.a: If the refrigerator is under 5 years old and the compressor is under warranty, repair makes sense. For older units without warranty, compare the repair cost to a new refrigerator.

title: Defrost Heater Error
shortDescription: The defrost heater is not functioning properly.
fullDescription: The Er dH error code indicates the defrost heater has failed to reach the required temperature during a defrost cycle. The defrost heater is a heating element located behind the evaporator coils in the freezer that melts frost buildup during scheduled defrost cycles. Without a working defrost heater, frost accumulates on the evaporator, eventually blocking airflow and causing cooling problems.

causes.cause: Defrost control board not initiating defrost cycle
Wiring issue in the defrost circuit

symptoms: Er dH displayed on the control panel
Heavy frost or ice on the freezer back wall
Refrigerator section not cooling adequately
Freezer still cold but refrigerator section is warm
Water pooling at the bottom of the freezer

fixSteps.title: Manual defrost first
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator and leave both doors open for 24 hours to fully defrost. Place towels to catch water. This restores normal operation temporarily while you diagnose.

fixSteps.title: Access the defrost components
fixSteps.instruction: After defrosting, remove the shelves and drawers from the freezer. Remove the back panel inside the freezer (held by screws or clips) to expose the evaporator coils and defrost heater.

fixSteps.title: Test the defrost heater
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the defrost heater wiring. Using a multimeter, test for continuity across the heater terminals. It should read 20-40 ohms. If open (no continuity), the heater has burned out and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test the defrost thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the defrost thermostat (bimetal) on the evaporator. At room temperature after defrosting, it should show continuity. If it shows no continuity at room temperature, it is stuck open and needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable accessing the evaporator area or if the defrost control board needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er dH defrost heater error fix

faq.q: Why is the refrigerator section warm but the freezer is still cold?
faq.a: The freezer cools first, and cold air is then distributed to the refrigerator by the evaporator fan. When frost blocks the evaporator, the limited cold air stays in the freezer and the refrigerator warms up.

faq.q: How often does the defrost cycle run?
faq.a: LG refrigerators typically defrost every 8-12 hours of compressor run time, for about 20-30 minutes each cycle.

faq.q: Can I manually trigger a defrost cycle?
faq.a: Some LG models allow forced defrost through a button sequence. Hold Refrigerator Temp and Ice Plus buttons simultaneously for 4 seconds to enter test mode, then select defrost.

title: Freezer Sensor Error
shortDescription: The freezer temperature sensor (thermistor) has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The Er FS error code indicates the freezer temperature sensor is providing abnormal readings or has failed. This sensor reports the freezer temperature to the control board, which uses this data to cycle the compressor on and off to maintain the correct temperature.

causes.cause: Loose or corroded sensor wiring connection

symptoms: Er FS displayed on the control panel
Freezer temperature not regulated properly
Freezer too cold or not cold enough
Compressor running continuously or not at all

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and plug back in. Transient sensor errors may clear with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Locate the freezer sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The freezer thermistor is typically located inside the freezer compartment, often clipped to the evaporator cover or wall. It is a small probe connected by two wires.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Follow the sensor wires to their connector. Disconnect and reconnect the connector to ensure good contact. Check for corrosion or damage.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor wiring. At room temperature (77F), the sensor should read approximately 5,000 ohms. At freezer temperature (0F), it should read approximately 16,000 ohms. Replace if readings are abnormal.

whenToCallPro: If sensor replacement does not clear the error, the control board may need diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er FS freezer sensor thermistor fix

faq.q: Can a bad sensor cause the freezer to be too cold?
faq.a: Yes, if the sensor reads warmer than actual, the control board will run the compressor more, making the freezer excessively cold.

faq.q: Is the sensor expensive?
faq.a: No, refrigerator thermistors are one of the cheapest parts at $8-20.

faq.q: Can I use the refrigerator while waiting for the part?
faq.a: The refrigerator may still cool, but temperature regulation will be inconsistent. Monitor food temperatures and use a separate thermometer.

title: Flow Sensor Error (Water Dispenser)
shortDescription: The water flow sensor in the dispenser system has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The Er gF error code indicates a problem with the water flow sensor that monitors water flow through the dispenser system. This sensor measures how much water is dispensed and communicates with the ice maker's fill valve. A malfunction can cause over-dispensing, under-dispensing, or ice maker fill problems.

causes.cause: Air trapped in the water line

symptoms: Er gF displayed on the control panel
Water dispenser not working or dispensing inconsistently
Ice maker not filling properly
Water dispenser runs for wrong amount of time

fixSteps.title: Replace the water filter
fixSteps.instruction: A clogged filter restricts flow and can confuse the flow sensor. Replace the water filter with a new genuine LG filter. After installing, flush 2-3 gallons of water through the dispenser.

fixSteps.title: Purge air from the water line
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the water dispenser for 5 seconds, release for 5 seconds, and repeat 10 times. This pushes any trapped air out of the water line that could cause false readings.

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and plug back in. This resets the flow sensor readings and calibration.

fixSteps.title: Replace the flow sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and water supply. The flow sensor is located in the water line inside the refrigerator, often behind the rear panel. Disconnect the water line fittings, remove the old sensor, and install the new one.

whenToCallPro: If the flow sensor is in a difficult-to-access location or if water line work makes you uncomfortable.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er gF water flow sensor error fix

faq.q: Does the water dispenser still work with Er gF?
faq.a: It may work intermittently or deliver incorrect amounts of water. The dispenser function varies by model when this sensor fails.

faq.q: Can a clogged filter cause Er gF?
faq.a: Yes, a severely clogged filter restricts water flow enough to trigger false readings from the flow sensor. Replace the filter every 6 months.

faq.q: Do I need to replace the filter with LG brand?
faq.a: LG recommends genuine LG filters for best performance. Compatible third-party filters exist but verify they meet NSF/ANSI standards.

title: Humidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The humidity sensor inside the refrigerator has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The Er HS error code indicates the humidity sensor has failed. Some LG refrigerators use humidity sensors to optimize moisture levels inside the crisper drawers and refrigerator compartment. A failed sensor can affect the freshness preservation system but typically does not affect basic cooling.

causes.cause: Sensor wiring connection loose or corroded

symptoms: Er HS displayed on the control panel
Smart humidity control not functioning
Produce may dry out or get too moist
Freshness indicators on display not working

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and plug back in. Sensor errors may clear with a power cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check the sensor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the humidity sensor (usually in the ceiling of the refrigerator compartment or near the crisper area). Check that its wiring connector is secure.

fixSteps.title: Clean or dry the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor area has condensation, gently clean and dry it. Moisture on the sensor electronics can cause false readings.

fixSteps.title: Replace the humidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after reset and cleaning, disconnect power and replace the sensor. Remove mounting clips or screws, disconnect the wiring, and install the new sensor.

whenToCallPro: Usually not needed for sensor replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er HS humidity sensor error fix

faq.q: Will the refrigerator still cool with Er HS?
faq.a: Yes, Er HS affects the humidity management features but does not impact basic cooling and freezing.

faq.q: What does the humidity sensor do?
faq.a: It monitors moisture levels to optimize the crisper drawer environment and smart freshness features.

faq.q: Is Er HS urgent?
faq.a: No, this is a low-priority error. The refrigerator functions normally for cooling. The smart humidity features just won't work optimally.

title: Ice Maker Sensor Error
shortDescription: The ice maker temperature or fill sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The Er IS error code indicates a sensor in the ice maker system is not functioning correctly. This could be the ice maker thermostat that detects when ice is frozen, the fill sensor that monitors water levels, or the ice level sensor that determines when the bin is full.
symptoms: Er IS displayed on the control panel
Ice maker not producing ice
Ice maker continuously produces ice (overflows bin)
Ice maker does not eject ice cubes

fixSteps.title: Reset the ice maker
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the ice maker reset button (usually on the underside of the ice maker unit). Press and hold it for 3-5 seconds. Wait 24 hours for ice production to resume.

fixSteps.title: Check the ice level sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the bin is not full but the sensor says it is, clean the infrared ice level sensor near the top of the ice bin area. Wipe it with a soft cloth to remove frost or ice.

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and plug back in. This resets all sensors and can clear transient errors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the ice maker assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the ice maker may need replacement as a complete unit. Disconnect power, remove the ice bin, disconnect the wiring harness and water line from the ice maker, remove mounting screws, and install the new unit.

whenToCallPro: If the ice maker assembly needs replacement and you are not comfortable disconnecting the water line.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er IS ice maker sensor error fix

faq.q: Can I still use the refrigerator with Er IS?
faq.a: Yes, cooling is not affected. Only ice production is impacted. You can use manual ice trays in the meantime.

faq.q: How do I reset the ice maker?
faq.a: Find the reset button on the underside of the ice maker unit. Press and hold it for about 5 seconds until you hear a chime. Wait 24 hours.

faq.q: How long does it take for ice maker to start after reset?
faq.a: Allow 24 hours after a reset for the ice maker to complete a full cycle and begin producing ice normally.

title: Refrigerator Fan Error
shortDescription: The refrigerator compartment fan motor is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: The Er rF error code indicates the fan motor that circulates cold air specifically within the refrigerator compartment has failed. Some LG models have a dedicated refrigerator compartment fan separate from the freezer evaporator fan. When this fan fails, the refrigerator section warms up while the freezer may remain cold.

causes.cause: Ice or frost blocking the fan blades
Control board not powering the fan

symptoms: Er rF displayed on the control panel
Refrigerator compartment is warm
Freezer is still cold
No air movement felt in the refrigerator when door is open
Food spoiling in the refrigerator section

fixSteps.title: Check for ice blocking the fan
fixSteps.instruction: Open the refrigerator and listen for the fan. If you hear no fan, check for frost or ice around the air vent areas inside the refrigerator. Ice buildup may be blocking the fan.

fixSteps.title: Defrost if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If ice is present, unplug the refrigerator and leave both doors open until all ice melts (12-24 hours). This clears any obstruction.

fixSteps.title: Access and test the fan
fixSteps.instruction: After defrosting, remove the refrigerator back panel or ceiling panel (varies by model) to access the fan. Plug in the refrigerator and observe if the fan runs. If not, disconnect power and test with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the fan motor mounting hardware, disconnect the wiring, and install the new motor. Transfer the fan blade if not included with the new motor.

whenToCallPro: If recurring ice buildup is causing fan failures, the defrost system needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er rF refrigerator fan error fix

faq.q: Why is only the refrigerator warm?
faq.a: The dedicated refrigerator fan circulates cold air from the freezer/evaporator to the refrigerator section. When it fails, cold air does not reach the refrigerator even though the freezer remains cold.

faq.q: Is my food safe?
faq.a: If the refrigerator is above 40F (4C), perishable food should be consumed or discarded within 4 hours per food safety guidelines. Use a thermometer to check.

faq.q: Can the fan be lubricated instead of replaced?
faq.a: Fan motors in refrigerators are typically sealed units that cannot be lubricated. Replacement is the correct repair for a failed motor.

title: Room Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The ambient room temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The Er rt error code indicates the room temperature (ambient) sensor is providing abnormal readings. This sensor monitors the temperature of the environment around the refrigerator, which the control board uses to adjust compressor operation for optimal efficiency. A failed sensor does not usually prevent cooling but may cause the compressor to cycle inefficiently.

causes.cause: Sensor exposed to heat source (direct sunlight, oven)

symptoms: Er rt displayed on the control panel
Compressor may run more or less than optimal
Slight temperature fluctuations
Energy efficiency reduced

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and plug back in. Sensor errors may clear with a power cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor placement
fixSteps.instruction: The ambient sensor is typically near the control board or at the back/top of the refrigerator. Ensure it is not near a heat source. Move the refrigerator away from direct sunlight, ovens, or heating vents.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the sensor wiring connection for looseness or corrosion. Reconnect firmly.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, replace the ambient temperature sensor. It is usually a simple two-wire sensor with a clip mount. At room temperature it should read approximately 5,000 ohms.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed for this low-priority sensor replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er rt room temperature sensor error

faq.q: Is Er rt serious?
faq.a: No, Er rt is a low-priority error. The refrigerator will continue to cool. It may just be slightly less energy efficient.

faq.q: Does room temperature affect my refrigerator?
faq.a: Yes, extreme room temperatures (very hot garage or very cold unheated space) can affect refrigerator performance. The sensor helps the control board compensate.

faq.q: Where should the refrigerator be located?
faq.a: LG recommends placing the refrigerator in an environment between 55F and 110F, away from direct sunlight and heat sources, with at least 2 inches of clearance on all sides.

title: Pantry/Drawer Sensor Error
shortDescription: The temperature sensor in the pantry drawer or specialty compartment has failed.
fullDescription: The Er SS error code indicates the temperature sensor in a specialty compartment (such as the pantry drawer, convertible zone, or custom chill drawer) has malfunctioned. This sensor allows the compartment to maintain a different temperature than the main refrigerator section. When it fails, the specialty compartment may not maintain its set temperature.
symptoms: Er SS displayed on the control panel
Specialty compartment not maintaining set temperature
Temperature display for the drawer shows incorrect reading
Items in the specialty compartment freezing or not cold enough

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and plug back in. The sensor reading may normalize after a reset.

fixSteps.title: Check the sensor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the specialty drawer and locate the temperature sensor (usually a small probe clipped inside the drawer housing). Check the wiring connection.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and test resistance with a multimeter. At refrigerator temperature (37F), expect approximately 7,000-8,000 ohms. Replace if readings are abnormal.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old sensor from its clip or mounting. Install the new sensor in the same position and reconnect the wiring.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed. Sensor replacement is straightforward.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er SS drawer sensor error fix

faq.q: Will the main refrigerator and freezer still work?
faq.a: Yes, Er SS only affects the specialty compartment. The main refrigerator and freezer continue to function normally.

faq.q: Can I store food in the drawer with Er SS?
faq.a: You can, but the temperature may not be accurately controlled. Use a separate thermometer to verify the temperature is safe for your food.

faq.q: Which models have specialty compartments?
faq.a: LG's InstaView, French door, and craft ice models often include specialty compartments like the Full-Convert Drawer or CustomChill drawer.

title: Communication Error Between Boards
shortDescription: The main control board and display board are not communicating properly.
fullDescription: The Er CH error code indicates a communication failure between the main control board (usually at the back of the refrigerator) and the display/UI board (on the door). The two boards communicate via a wiring harness that runs through the door hinge area. This harness can be damaged by repeated door opening or pinched during installation.

causes.cause: Damaged wire harness through the door hinge

symptoms: Er CH displayed on the control panel
Display showing incorrect information
Button presses not registering
Temperature settings not being applied

fixSteps.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and plug back in. Communication errors can be temporary glitches.

fixSteps.title: Check the door hinge wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Open the refrigerator door and inspect the wiring harness that passes through the top hinge area. Look for pinched, frayed, or broken wires. This is the most common cause of Er CH.

fixSteps.title: Reseat board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top hinge cover and check the wiring connectors. Also open the control panel on the door and the main board housing at the back to reseat all ribbon cable and wiring connections.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the harness
fixSteps.instruction: If wires in the door hinge area are damaged, the wiring harness needs replacement. This involves removing the door, threading the new harness through the hinge, and reconnecting at both ends.

whenToCallPro: If the wiring harness through the door hinge needs replacement, or if you suspect a control board failure.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er CH communication error wire harness fix

faq.q: Will the refrigerator still cool with Er CH?
faq.a: It depends. If the main board can still operate the compressor independently, cooling may continue. However, temperature settings may not be applied correctly.

faq.q: Why do the door hinge wires break?
faq.a: The wires flex every time the door opens and closes. Over years of use, the repeated bending can cause wire fatigue and breakage, especially at the hinge point.

faq.q: Can I fix broken hinge wires?
faq.a: Individual wires can be spliced, but replacing the entire harness is recommended for reliability. The harness is designed for the specific routing through the hinge.

title: Door Switch Error / Dispenser Switch Error
shortDescription: A door switch or dispenser switch is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: The Er dS error code indicates a door switch or dispenser switch is not functioning properly. Door switches detect when doors are open or closed, controlling interior lights and fan operation. Dispenser switches control the water and ice dispensing functions. A stuck or failed switch can cause lighting issues, fan problems, or dispensing malfunctions.

causes.cause: Faulty door switch stuck in open or closed position
Control board switch input circuit failure

symptoms: Er dS displayed on the control panel
Interior lights stay on or off regardless of door position
Evaporator fan does not turn off when door is opened
Water or ice dispenser not functioning

fixSteps.title: Check the door switches
fixSteps.instruction: Press the door switches manually (small plunger buttons in the door frame). The interior lights should turn off and the fan should stop. If a switch does not click or does not affect the lights, it may be stuck.

fixSteps.title: Clean the switch area
fixSteps.instruction: Dirt and food residue can prevent the door switch plunger from moving freely. Clean around the switch with a damp cloth. Press the switch several times to free it if sticky.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the door switch (usually held by a single clip or screw). Test with a multimeter for continuity when pressed and no continuity when released. Replace if faulty.

fixSteps.title: Check the dispenser switches
fixSteps.instruction: If the error relates to the dispenser, test the dispenser switches and lever mechanism. These are accessed by removing the dispenser front panel. Test each switch for proper operation.

whenToCallPro: If the dispenser module needs replacement, which involves more disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er dS door switch error fix

faq.q: Can a stuck door switch waste energy?
faq.a: Yes, if the switch is stuck telling the refrigerator the door is closed, the interior light stays off but the fan runs when it shouldn't, and vice versa. This wastes energy.

faq.q: How many door switches does my refrigerator have?
faq.a: Most LG French door refrigerators have a door switch on each door, plus switches for the freezer drawer. Some also have magnetic reed switches.

faq.q: Is a door switch the same as a door alarm?
faq.a: They are related but different. The door switch detects open/closed status for controlling lights and fans. The door alarm uses this information to beep when a door is left open.

title: Water Flow Error
shortDescription: The refrigerator detects insufficient water flow to the ice maker or dispenser.
fullDescription: The Er FL error code indicates the refrigerator is detecting low or no water flow to the dispenser or ice maker. This could be due to a clogged water filter, frozen water line, closed water supply valve, or a faulty water inlet valve.

causes.cause: Frozen water supply line inside the refrigerator
Water supply valve partially or fully closed

symptoms: Er FL displayed on the control panel
Water dispenser produces little or no water
Ice maker not filling or producing ice
Slow water flow from dispenser

fixSteps.title: Replace the water filter
fixSteps.instruction: A clogged filter is the most common cause. Replace the water filter with a new genuine LG filter (LT1000P or LT700P depending on model). Flush 2-3 gallons through the dispenser after installing.

fixSteps.title: Check the water supply valve
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the water supply valve (usually behind the refrigerator or under the kitchen sink). Ensure it is fully open.

fixSteps.title: Thaw frozen water line
fixSteps.instruction: If the water line inside the refrigerator is frozen, unplug the refrigerator for 2 hours or use a hair dryer on low to thaw the line. The line often freezes where it enters the back of the freezer.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and water supply. Remove the inlet valve access panel at the back bottom. Test the valve solenoid with a multimeter (500-1500 ohms). Replace if faulty.

whenToCallPro: If the water inlet valve needs replacement or if a frozen line keeps recurring.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er FL water flow error fix filter

faq.q: How often should I replace the water filter?
faq.a: LG recommends replacing the water filter every 6 months for optimal flow and water quality.

faq.q: Why does the water line freeze?
faq.a: The water line can freeze if the freezer temperature is set too low (below -4F), if the line contacts the evaporator coils, or if the line is kinked creating a point where water sits and freezes.

faq.q: Can I use the refrigerator without a filter?
faq.a: Yes, most LG models have a filter bypass plug that allows operation without a filter. However, water will not be filtered.

title: Door Gap / Alignment Error
shortDescription: The refrigerator doors are not properly aligned, creating a gap.
fullDescription: The Er 67 error code indicates the refrigerator has detected a door alignment issue where the doors are not sealing properly. On French door models, the doors may have become misaligned, creating a gap between them or between the door and the cabinet. This allows warm air to enter, affecting cooling efficiency and causing frost buildup.

causes.cause: French doors have shifted out of alignment
Door hinges are loose or worn
Refrigerator not level causing door sag

symptoms: Er 67 displayed on the control panel
Visible gap between French doors
Condensation or frost on the door edges
Door alarm sounding intermittently
Higher energy consumption

fixSteps.title: Level the refrigerator
fixSteps.instruction: Place a bubble level on top of the refrigerator. Adjust the front leveling legs by turning them clockwise (to raise) or counterclockwise (to lower). The refrigerator should be level side-to-side and slightly tilted back.

fixSteps.title: Adjust the French door alignment
fixSteps.instruction: If one door is higher than the other, adjust using the hex bolt on the lower hinge of the higher door. Turn the bolt clockwise to lower the door or counterclockwise to raise it. Make small adjustments and check alignment.

fixSteps.title: Check the door gaskets
fixSteps.instruction: Close the doors and inspect the gaskets around the entire perimeter. Use a dollar bill test: close the door on a bill and try to slide it out. If it slides easily, the gasket is not sealing. Replace if deformed or torn.

fixSteps.title: Tighten door hinges
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Check all door hinge screws for tightness. On French door models, the upper and lower hinges can loosen over time, especially on the side that is opened most often. Tighten all hinge screws.

whenToCallPro: If hinge replacement is needed or if the door frame is warped.
videoSearchQuery: LG refrigerator Er 67 door alignment gap fix French door

faq.q: Why do French doors go out of alignment?
faq.a: Heavy items on the door shelves, frequent opening, and minor settling of the floor can all cause French doors to shift over time. Regular adjustment is normal.

faq.q: What is the dollar bill test?
faq.a: Close the door on a dollar bill at various points around the gasket. If you can easily pull the bill out, the gasket is not sealing properly at that point.

faq.q: Does Er 67 affect cooling?
faq.a: Yes, a door gap allows warm humid air to enter the refrigerator, making the compressor work harder and potentially causing frost buildup.
